title: lithocarpus curranii
intro: Lithocarpus curranii, also known as Philippine tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, native to the Philippines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lithocarpus curranii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
